title: Number of Connected Components (Union-Find)
problem_statement: Count the number of connected components in an undirected graph with n nodes and given edges.
intuition: Use union-find to merge nodes connected by edges and count distinct roots for components.

approaches.name: Union-Find count
approaches.description: Initialize each node as its own parent, union endpoints, then count unique parents after path compression.
approaches.why_not: DFS/BFS also works, but union-find is simpler for repeated edge merges.

interview_qa.q: What does each root represent after union operations?
interview_qa.a: A root identifies one connected component.
It flattens the parent tree, improving future find operations.
